description.en: The error response body. This should be an HTTP response body bytestring. It defaults to the plain text, “A server error occurred. Please contact the administrator.”

excerpt.es: The error response body. This should be an HTTP response body bytestring. It defaults to the plain text, “A server error occurred. Please contact the administrator.”
excerpt.en: The error response body. This should be an HTTP response body bytestring. It defaults to the plain text, “A server error occurred. Please contact the administrator.”

description.en: The HTTP headers used for error responses. This should be a list of WSGI response headers ((name, value) tuples), as described in PEP 3333. The default list just sets the content type to text/plain.

excerpt.es: The HTTP headers used for error responses. This should be a list of WSGI response headers ((name, value) tuples), as described in PEP 3333. The default list just sets the content type to text/plain.
excerpt.en: The HTTP headers used for error responses. This should be a list of WSGI response headers ((name, value) tuples), as described in PEP 3333. The default list just sets the content type to text/plain.

description.en: The HTTP status used for error responses. This should be a status string as defined in PEP 3333; it defaults to a 500 code and message.

excerpt.es: The HTTP status used for error responses. This should be a status string as defined in PEP 3333; it defaults to a 500 code and message.
excerpt.en: The HTTP status used for error responses. This should be a status string as defined in PEP 3333; it defaults to a 500 code and message.

description.en: A typing.Protocol describing a WSGI Error Stream.

excerpt.en: A typing.Protocol describing a WSGI Error Stream.

description.en: This method is a WSGI application to generate an error page for the user. It is only invoked if an error occurs before headers are sent to the client. This method can access the current error using sys.exception(), and should pass that information to start_response when calling it (as described in the “Error Handling” section of PEP 3333). In particular, the start_response callable should follow the StartResponse protocol. The default implementation just uses the error_status, error_headers, ...
